message:
chore(beads): review-pass-6 — 4 HIGH release/collab/reverse-conformance gaps

Continued /testing-conformance-harnesses + repo hygiene audit.
Workspace baseline steady (3171 passed / 0 failed / 13 ignored).
No shipped-code regression.

  frankenpandas-kmbc [HIGH] ci: feature matrix never tested.
                            fp-runtime + fp-conformance expose
                            `asupersync` feature but ci.yml only
                            runs `cargo check --all-targets`
                            (default features). `cargo check
                            --no-default-features`, `cargo check
                            --all-features`, and per-feature
                            `cargo test -p fp-runtime --features
                            asupersync` are all missing. Upcoming
                            fd90 SQL backend epic plans 3-4 new
                            features — matrix gate must ship
                            first or every combo break is a
                            potential regression.

  frankenpandas-4clx [HIGH] release: 12 crates at 0.1.0 with no
                            publish workflow or semver policy.
                            No `publish = false`, no
                            workspace.package version inheritance,
                            no release.yml, no CHANGELOG policy.
                            First release is structurally
                            impossible without 12 manual
                            publishes in topological order plus
                            version-pin rewrites. The diic PyO3
                            bindings epic depends on core crates
                            being publishable.

  frankenpandas-6d5s [HIGH] collab: no CODEOWNERS / PR template /
                            CONTRIBUTING.md / ISSUE_TEMPLATE /
                            CODE_OF_CONDUCT despite an active
                            multi-agent swarm. Evidence: 20+
                            commits across 6 hours from multiple
                            agents today, pre-commit agent-mail
                            hooks in place, br-iy8u doctrine bead
                            already noted cod bundles co-landed
                            work. The swarm has oral conventions
                            but no mechanized review-routing or
                            PR-checklist enforcement.

  frankenpandas-kdwn [HIGH] conformance: no reverse-conformance.
                            Every conformance path is
                            pandas→Rust. Nothing tests
                            pandas-reads-frankenpandas-output.
                            Same-impl round-trips (assert_*_roundtrip
                            helpers) would pass even on a file
                            format frankenpandas encodes in a way
                            only frankenpandas can decode. A user
                            piping fp-io output into pd.read_*
                            has no conformance guarantee. Fix:
                            oracle/reverse_oracle.py + packets_reverse/
                            fixture dir + assert_reverse_round_trip
                            comparator.

Interlock with prior passes:
- kmbc (feature matrix) + fd90 (SQL backend epic) — the epic ships
  3-4 new features; matrix must gate first.
- 4clx (release workflow) + 1d9y (nightly pin) + 36qc (cargo-audit)
  + 0l5r (cross-platform CI) — all four gates green = first
  release feasible; any missing = release-time surprise.
- 6d5s (CODEOWNERS) + iy8u (doctrine) — enforces what iy8u
  articulated.
- kdwn (reverse conformance) + d6xa (CI pandas install) + liai
  (differential fuzz) — three complementary testing dimensions:
  forward math (liai), serialize-then-parse (kdwn), and runtime
  enforcement of the pipeline (d6xa).

Running session totals after 6 review passes:
  2 CRITICAL
  21 HIGH
  4 MEDIUM
  1 LOW
= 28 review beads total.

Each bead carries concrete fix shape + blast radius + prerequisite
interlocks. Zero shipped-code bugs. Workspace stays 3171/0/13
with 0 clippy warnings across all 6 passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-4clx","title":"[HIGH] release: 12 crates at 0.1.0 with no publish workflow or semver policy — library is structurally un-releasable","description":"Release / publish workflow + versioning audit.

**Observation.**
- All 12 crates sit at version = "0.1.0" (verified via grep). No crate has been bumped since the workspace was initialized.
- No `publish = false` set anywhere. Cargo's default is `publish = true`, which means any developer running `cargo publish -p fp-types` today would push 0.1.0 to crates.io. A moment later, `cargo publish -p fp-columnar` fails because fp-columnar has `fp-types = { path = "../fp-types" }` and path-dependencies can't be published. The publish ordering is undocumented.
- .github/workflows/ has exactly ONE file: ci.yml. No release.yml, no publish.yml, no tag-driven workflow.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` section per crate — there's a workspace-root CHANGELOG but no per-crate versioning story.
- No semver policy documented. The workspace edition is "2024" (Rust 2024 edition, nightly-only), so MSRV is unpinned — but semver needs a user-facing stability story.

**Consequence.**
- First release is impossible today without manual ceremony: 12 Cargo.toml bumps, dependency-path rewrite to `version = "..."` pinned cross-crate refs, cargo publish each in topological order, tag the commit, update Cargo.lock, repeat across all downstream crates. Any misstep breaks the publish.
- frankenpandas-diic bead (PyO3 bindings) depends on the core crates being publishable — the Python wheel pipeline needs `frankenpandas = "X.Y.Z"` from crates.io.
- Multi-agent swarm adds commits faster than any manual release process can keep up. If release is manual, releases never happen; users stay on git dependencies.
- Security fixes (future CVE patches) can't ship quickly because the pipeline doesn't exist.

**Severity.** HIGH — the product is structurally un-releasable today despite being 3171-tests-green. README markets "Add to your Cargo.toml: frankenpandas = { path = 'crates/frankenpandas' }" which is a path dep, not a released version. Users can't consume the library the way the README implies.

**Fix shape:**

1. Workspace-level versioning:
   - Every crate's Cargo.toml [package] version inherits from workspace via `version.workspace = true`.
   - Single source of truth in root Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version = "0.1.0" → bumped once per release.
2. Semver policy in CONTRIBUTING.md (sibling bead): pre-1.0 = minor bumps may break, patch bumps won't; post-1.0 = strict semver.
3. Cross-crate dep version pinning: [workspace.dependencies] gets one fp-types = { version = "0.1.0", path = "crates/fp-types" } entry each, so published crates carry a versioned dep and path-lookup happens only during local dev.
4. Release workflow `.github/workflows/release.yml`:
   - Trigger: git tag matching `v*`.
   - Steps: validate semver bump matches commit messages, run full CI suite, `cargo publish -p <crate>` in topological order (fp-types → fp-columnar → fp-index → fp-runtime → fp-types → fp-expr → fp-groupby → fp-join → fp-io → fp-conformance → frankenpandas), attach CHANGELOG.md diff to GitHub Release.
5. `cargo-release` (external tool) or `release-plz` (GitHub Action) automates the topological publish. Install in CI; configure via release-plz.toml.
6. Per-crate CHANGELOG files auto-generated by release-plz from conventional-commit prefixes (feat/fix/chore/etc — the prefixes are already in use based on recent git log).

Blast radius:
- 12 Cargo.toml edits (version.workspace = true flip, +1 line each).
- workspace Cargo.toml: add [workspace.package] version, add [workspace.dependencies] entries for each fp-* crate.
- 1 new .github/workflows/release.yml (~100 lines).
- 1 new release-plz.toml (~30 lines).
- 1 new section in CONTRIBUTING.md (covered by sibling CODEOWNERS bead).

Prerequisites:
- br-frankenpandas-36qc (cargo-audit) + br-frankenpandas-0l5r (cross-platform CI) ideally green before 0.1.0 ships — you don't want to publish a 0.1.0 that's untested on Windows.
- br-frankenpandas-1d9y (nightly pin) — MSRV claim can't happen without a pinned toolchain.

Once all prerequisites + this bead land, first release becomes `git tag v0.1.0 && git push --tags`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:30:47.204682706Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:30:47.204682706Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["publish","release","review","semver"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-6d5s","title":"[HIGH] collab: no CODEOWNERS / PR template / CONTRIBUTING.md despite active multi-agent swarm — zero review-routing infrastructure","description":"Contributor / collaboration infrastructure audit.

**Observation.** `.github/` contains exactly one directory (workflows/) and nothing else. Confirmed absences:
  .github/CODEOWNERS                      — no review routing
  .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md        — no PR checklist
  .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/                 — no issue templates
  /CONTRIBUTING.md                        — no contributor guide
  /CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md                     — no code of conduct
  /SECURITY.md                            — no disclosure (br-8k1i covers separately)

**Context.** This repository is running an ACTIVE multi-agent development swarm — that's not speculation. Evidence from today's git log alone:
- 20+ commits across 6 hours from multiple agents (cc-pandas + Dicklesworthstone + parallel workers).
- br-frankenpandas-iy8u: the original bead was a doctrine note about agents bundling co-landed work with misleading titles. Closed today by c9e9fff ("docs(workflow): require pre-commit diff stat and co-landed bead IDs").
- br-frankenpandas-lxhr (monolith bead): cites rebase conflicts between parallel agents on fp-frame/src/lib.rs as a primary pain point.
- Pre-commit hooks at `.git/hooks/hooks.d/pre-commit/50-agent-mail.py` prove the swarm coordinates through agent-mail infra.

Despite the swarm being active, there is NO documented process for:
- Which agent reviews which area of code (no CODEOWNERS).
- What a PR must include before merge (no PR template).
- How external contributors (humans) onboard (no CONTRIBUTING.md).
- What behavior is expected in issues / PRs (no CODE_OF_CONDUCT).
- How scientific / pandas-behavior-divergence discussions should be classified (no issue template distinguishing bug / feature / conformance-question / docs).

**Consequences already visible:**
- br-iy8u root cause (cod-style commits bundling co-landed work) persists as oral doctrine rather than enforceable CODEOWNERS routing.
- New slice-1 child bead (1zzp.1) had two agents working the same file simultaneously; the CODEOWNERS + PR template would have routed one to review the other's draft rather than both editing in parallel.
- Contributor onboarding is zero-documented: a new agent (this cc-pandas session is literally "fresh cc on new account" per the opening user message) had to reconstruct workflow from context clues.

**Severity.** HIGH — infrastructure that compounds across every PR. Every day without it, the swarm eats overhead re-discovering which file to edit and which agent to block on.

**Fix shape:**

1. `.github/CODEOWNERS`:
   - `/crates/fp-types/ @core-types-owner` (or an agent alias like `@cc-frankenpandas-types`)
   - `/crates/fp-frame/ @frame-domain`
   - `/crates/fp-io/ @io-domain`
   - `/crates/fp-conformance/ @conformance-domain`
   - `/.github/workflows/ @ci-owners`
   - `/.beads/ @scrum-bot` (if there's a bead-curator agent)
   - Auto-routes PR review requests; works with the existing multi-agent workflow.

2. `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`:
   - Required: linked bead ID (br-frankenpandas-*)
   - Required: conformance / fuzz / performance impact checkbox
   - Required: `git diff --stat` summary (directly addresses br-iy8u doctrine) — autopopulate via the pre-commit hook already in place
   - Required: which live_oracle_* tests the PR touches / exercises
   - Required: acceptance criteria from the child bead

3. `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md`, `feature_request.md`, `conformance_divergence.md`:
   - Conformance divergences get their own template because they flow to DISCREPANCIES.md, not to a regular bug tracker.

4. `CONTRIBUTING.md`:
   - How to install rch + cargo-fuzz + pandas oracle.
   - How to file a bead (`br create --no-db ...` per today's doctrine).
   - AGENTS.md link for swarm-specific workflow.
   - How to run conformance (`rch exec -- cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib`).
   - How to regenerate fixtures (once br-boyr ships the pipeline).
   - Semver and release expectations (once br-4clx ships).

5. `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`: boilerplate Contributor Covenant. Five minutes to add.

Blast radius: 5 new files, no source code changes. Each file is independently useful — can ship as 5 small PRs instead of one.

Related:
- br-frankenpandas-iy8u (workflow doctrine) — already closed via the c9e9fff commit that added pre-commit diff-stat requirement. This bead captures the codepath-level enforcement (CODEOWNERS), the human-facing documentation (CONTRIBUTING), and the PR-time enforcement (template) that the doctrine bead acknowledged were needed.
- br-frankenpandas-8k1i (SECURITY.md) — sibling file in the /github root; coordinate rollout.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:31:37.124310007Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:31:37.124310007Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["collab","infrastructure","review","swarm"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-kdwn","title":"[HIGH] conformance: no reverse-conformance — we never test 'pandas reads frankenpandas output' even though interop is the user story","description":"Reverse-conformance audit (pandas reads frankenpandas output).

**Observation.** The entire conformance harness is ONE-DIRECTIONAL: pandas_oracle.py produces expected values from pandas, our Rust impl matches. There is no test path in the reverse direction — frankenpandas writes a Parquet/Feather/Excel/CSV/JSON artifact, pandas reads it, assertions confirm the values pandas sees match what frankenpandas intended.

Verified via grep: nowhere in crates/fp-conformance/ does any test call pandas with data produced by frankenpandas. The oracle path always GENERATES from pandas; never VALIDATES by having pandas consume our output. Comments like `/// Matches pd.read_csv(...)` are prose, not runtime checks.

Round-trip tests that DO exist (e.g. assert_csv_roundtrip, assert_parquet_roundtrip, assert_feather_roundtrip at fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:5019+) are same-impl round-trips: frankenpandas-writes → frankenpandas-reads. If our writer encodes a Parquet file that is technically malformed but our reader is just as lenient, the round-trip passes but every OTHER Parquet consumer (pandas, DuckDB, Polars) rejects the file. User silently can't interop.

**Concrete failure modes this gap allows:**
- Parquet: we write a column with logical type TIMESTAMP(MILLIS, UTC) but the physical schema claims INT64 without annotation. Our reader back-infers from context; pandas's pyarrow-backed read sees unannotated INT64 and reads it as nanoseconds-since-epoch, producing year-1970 timestamps.
- Arrow IPC: we write a schema with a custom_metadata key that pandas's pyarrow can't parse; pandas raises on open.
- Excel: we write a shared-strings table that works in our round-trip but LibreOffice Calc rejects; pandas routes through openpyxl and fails.
- CSV: we write NaN as the string "nan" (lowercase) but pandas defaults to "NaN"; pandas reads our "nan" as the literal string, not a missing value.
- Parquet: we write column-level nullability = true but always fill nulls as 0 instead of using the bitmap; pandas reads zeros as real integers, changing semantics.

**Skill rule violated:** Pattern 3 Round-Trip Conformance (the skill template for cross-impl round-trips, not same-impl). Explicit skill text: "1. We must be able to parse reference output. 2. We re-serialize. 3. Reference must be able to parse our output. 4. Parsed values must match."

We have step 1 (our-reader-on-pandas-output covered by fixture mode). We do NOT have step 3 or 4 (pandas-reader-on-our-output).

**Severity.** HIGH — pandas interop is the PRIMARY user story ("clean-room Rust reimplementation of pandas API"). Without reverse conformance, users who actually try to pipe frankenpandas → pandas hit divergence that our test suite declares impossible.

**Fix shape:**

1. `oracle/reverse_oracle.py` — new Python harness. Accepts JSON of {file_bytes_base64, format, schema} and runs pandas' read path. Emits {parsed_frame_json, errors}.
2. New conformance fixtures: `fixtures/packets_reverse/` with 50-100 cases that exercise each writer. Schema: `{frame_input, write_format, expected_pandas_parsed}`. We write the frame via fp-io, pipe bytes to reverse_oracle.py, compare pandas's parsed output against expected_pandas_parsed.
3. Rust-side: `assert_reverse_round_trip(frame: &DataFrame, format: WriteFormat) -> Result<(), HarnessError>`. Writes frame, calls reverse_oracle.py subprocess, compares parsed output via semantic_eq.
4. Per IO format, 5-10 canonical test cases (empty frame, single column, nulls, special scalars NaN/Inf/NaT, unicode strings, large ints, timezones).
5. Extend COVERAGE.md (once br-n609 auto-generator lands) with a "Reverse-conformance" section per format.

Gate placement:
- Phase 1: new Rust test suite `crates/fp-conformance/tests/reverse_conformance.rs`. Runs under `cargo test -p fp-conformance` once pandas is installed in CI (br-d6xa prerequisite).
- Phase 2: reverse oracle gets its own fuzz target (differential: we-write → they-read, assert no panic and assert semantic_eq).

Blast radius: new Python file, new Rust test file, ~50-100 new small fixtures. No changes to existing fixtures.

Depends on br-frankenpandas-d6xa for CI-side pandas install. +{"id":"frankenpandas-kmbc","title":"[HIGH] ci: feature matrix never tested — asupersync path (and upcoming SQL backends) can silently break on main","description":"Feature-matrix audit.

**Observation.** Two fp-* crates expose Cargo features:
  fp-runtime/Cargo.toml:   [features] asupersync = ["dep:asupersync"]
  fp-conformance/Cargo.toml: [features] asupersync = ["fp-runtime/asupersync"]

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs a single `cargo check --all-targets` (line ~27) which enables DEFAULT features only. There is no `cargo check --no-default-features`, no `cargo check --features asupersync`, no combinatorial `cargo hack check --feature-powerset`.

**Consequence.** The asupersync code path is never exercised in CI. Earlier UPGRADE_LOG.md entry (2026-04-22 asupersync bump from 0.2 → 0.3) noted `rch exec -- cargo test -p fp-runtime --features asupersync` was run locally — good — but it's a one-off, not a sustained gate. A regression that breaks compilation with `--features asupersync` would land on main green.

Additional miss: the workspace's `default = []` on fp-conformance means feature-on testing is opt-in. A user who enables asupersync via a downstream Cargo.toml hits code paths CI has never verified.

Future growth path is worse: br-frankenpandas-fd90 plans `sql-sqlite` / `sql-postgresql` / `sql-mysql` Cargo features for SQL backend gating. Without feature-matrix CI in place now, those land into a pipeline that silently allows each new feature combination to break compilation.

**Severity.** HIGH — every feature added without matrix gating compounds the risk. The "n features → 2^n combinations" scaling is already present (asupersync × upcoming SQL backends). Gating it today costs ~1 CI minute; gating it retroactively after a broken release is expensive in user trust.

**Fix shape:**

Phase 1 (minimum):
1. ci.yml adds three explicit builds:
     cargo check --workspace --no-default-features
     cargo check --workspace --all-features
     cargo check --workspace                              (current, default)
2. Same three against `cargo test` for the affected crates:
     cargo test -p fp-runtime --no-default-features
     cargo test -p fp-runtime --features asupersync
     cargo test -p fp-conformance --features asupersync

Phase 2 (recommended):
3. Install `cargo-hack` and run `cargo hack check --workspace --feature-powerset --no-dev-deps` (powerset of non-dev features). Catches every 2^n combo. Today (2 features) this is 4 builds; when fd90 lands it becomes 32 builds per crate.
4. Add `cargo hack check` to a separate CI job that runs in parallel with the main test job so it doesn't extend wall-clock time.

Blast radius: ci.yml (+~15 lines), no source code changes. Runs ~30-60s additional CI time for Phase 1; ~2-3 min for Phase 2.

Related:
- br-frankenpandas-fd90 (SQL backend epic) will ship 3-4 new features and needs matrix gating from day one.
- br-frankenpandas-36qc (cargo-audit) runs in the same CI but is orthogonal; matrix gate doesn't duplicate it.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:30:03.232634259Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:30:03.232634259Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build-matrix","ci","features","review"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-kusq","title":"pandas: DataFrame.pivot_table(margins=True, aggfunc='std'|'var') parity","description":"Python signature: pd.DataFrame.pivot_table(values=..., index=..., columns=..., aggfunc='std'|'var', margins=True).
